content.mi:
Te Toka Tapu

Kei te taha o tōna kāinga tētahi toka teitei, koi hoki, e tū ana i runga ake i te wai pēnei i tētahi kaitiaki e mātaki ana i te raorao.

Ko Te Toka-a-Houmea tēnei.

I tipu te harakeke i ōna taha, he mea whakatapua e ngā kawa a Te Tahi.

I konei ia i karakia ai,

i konei ia i tuku ai i āna inoi ki ngā atua tawhito,

i konei ia i kawe ai i te kaha o tōna mana.
content.en:
The Sacred Rock

Near his home stood a tall and jagged rock, rising high above the water like a guardian watching over the valley.

This was Te Toka-a-Houmea.

Harakeke grew from its sides, made sacred by Te Tahi’s rituals.

It was here he performed karakia,

here he offered prayers to ancient atua,

here he held the weight of his mana.
